curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: changing the subject of a formula using inverse operations
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Rearrange formulae to change the subject by using inverse operations and maintaining equality on both sides, progressing from simple products/quotients to multi-step formulas used in science and geometry. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can we change the subject of a formula without breaking the equality?

knowing_world.focus: Rearranging a formula means isolating a chosen variable by undoing operations in a logical order while keeping both sides balanced (doing the same thing to each side). :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
key_concepts: Balance principle (do the same to both sides)
activities: Start simple: rearrange A = bh to make h the subject.
Moderate example: rearrange s = ut + (1/2)at^2 to make a chosen variable the subject.
Quick check: rearrange v = u + at to make t the subject.
Quick check: make r the subject in V = πr^2h.

human_story.focus: From calculating interest rates to finding heights from volume, rearranging formulae helps solve real problems by treating equations like puzzles: you have relationships, and you find a path to the unknown. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

expressive_self.focus: Students make the logic visible through colour, visuals, and storytelling so rearranging feels like a traceable sequence rather than a mystery. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}
activities: Create a poster showing a rearranged formula step-by-step using visuals.
Design a character who rearranges formulas to solve mysteries.
Use colour coding to track steps in your rearrangement.

designed_world.focus: Students use a drag-and-drop tool to rearrange real-world formulas, reset for new examples, and optionally turn it into a timed challenge. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}
tools: Canva embed: drag-and-drop rearranging formulae tool (with reset)
activities: Start simple and level up through harder formulae.
Use the reset button to generate fresh formulae for practice.
Challenge a peer to beat your time while staying accurate.
design_challenges: Create a set of 6 formulae to rearrange (mixed difficulty) and provide an answer key with clear steps and signposting of inverse operations.

regulation_supports: Rearrange your body: stand for variables, crouch for numbers.
Move as if shifting terms across an invisible '=' sign to reinforce “balance both sides.” :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

pathways.name: Final Task - Create and Rearrange a Real-World Formula
pathways.focus: Create a real-world formula problem and rearrange it in at least two different ways, explaining each step and using the new formula in context. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}
products: One real-world formula problem you created (e.g., speed, volume, finance)
Two different rearrangements (two different subjects) with clear steps
A worked example using your rearranged formula in a practical context
A reflection on what made certain formulas harder to rearrange than others
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding: Explains each step and why inverse operations are valid
Application: Uses the rearranged formula correctly in a practical context
Independence & Reflection: Reflects on difficulty, strategy, and mistakes caught

required: Solve linear equations using inverse operations
Confidence with fractions and dividing by terms
Comfort with basic algebra notation and simplifying expressions

builds_toward: Rearranging more complex multi-step formulae in science and maths
Algebraic manipulation for modelling and problem solving
